name: libmath-base85-perl
title: libmath-base85-perl
summary: Perl extension for base 85 numbers, as referenced by RFC 1924
description:
Perl extension for base 85 numbers, as referenced by RFC 1924
RFC 1924 describes a compact, fixed-size representation of IPv6 addresses
which uses a base 85 number system. Math::Base85 handles some of the uglier
details of it.
.
The base 85 numbers (from 0 to 84) are as follows:
.
0..9 A..Z a..z ! # $ % & ( ) * + - ; < = > ? @ ^ _ ` { | } ~
.
At the moment, there's not much in this module. But it should be sufficient
for the purposes of RFC 1924.
.
This module has a variable called $Math::Base85::base85_digits, which is a
string containing the digits of the base 85 alphabet from lowest (0) to
highest (~), in that order.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libmath-base85-perl because Perl extension for base 85 numbers, as referenced by RFC 1924.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libmath-base85-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libmath-base85-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libmath-base85-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
